title: Review: Sources reading experience
description: Review all changes from the developer beads for correctness and convention compliance. Check: ArticleFeed enum cases and computed properties match spec exactly; APIClient query-item construction (source_id vs view, unread_only omission when false, cursor omission when nil); ArticleRowView importance pill thresholds and read/unread dimming; ArticlePagerView startIndex wiring; ArticleListView isCancellation benign handling, ListPreferences key names, duplicate-load guard, first-page limit:15 / subsequent limit:25, in-view header scrolls (not pinned); SourcesView sections order and NavigationLink destinations. Verify Liquid Glass conventions, SF Symbols usage, semantic colors only, Dynamic Type, accessibility labels, 44pt tap targets, no APIs newer than iOS 26.
acceptance_criteria: All new files reviewed against spec requirements
ArticleFeed properties and query mappings verified correct
APIClient query-item construction verified (param presence/absence)
isCancellation errors confirmed benign in ArticleListView
ListPreferences keys correctly named and not duplicated in a parallel store
Infinite scroll guard against duplicate concurrent loads verified
Liquid Glass conventions present across all views
Accessibility labels on all interactive elements
Tap targets >= 44x44pt
No hardcoded hex colors; no APIs newer than iOS 26

handoff_summary.completed: Reviewed all 9 changed source files against spec-7bb21007 and the bead acceptance criteria. ArticleFeed enum cases, IDs, computed properties, and query mappings are correct. APIClient.getArticles builds query items exactly per spec (source_id vs view, sort always sent, unread_only omitted when false, cursor omitted when nil, limit always sent). ArticleListView phase state machine is correct; isCancellation is handled as benign; first-page limit is 15, subsequent pages 25; isLoadingMore guard prevents duplicate concurrent loads; in-view header is a plain List row (scrolls with content, not pinned); GlassEffectContainer wraps the List. ArticleRowView importance pill thresholds (≥80 red / ≥50 orange / else secondary) match ArticleDetailView verbatim as specified; read/unread dimming uses primary/secondary. ArticlePagerView wires startIndex via State(initialValue:) in init; pager is bounded to the provided articles slice with no additional loading. ListPreferences uses keys aggregator.articlesSort and aggregator.articlesUnreadOnly (aggregator. prefix convention, correct defaults, not a parallel store). SourcesView renders Feeds section always in loaded state, Sources section conditionally when non-empty, NavigationLink destinations correct, accessibility labels on all interactive rows. ArticleFeedTests covers all three spec-required test cases including cursor percent-encoding. No hardcoded hex colors; SF Symbols semantically appropriate; Dynamic Type respected via system font styles; tap targets adequate via standard NavigationLink row heights.
handoff_summary.remaining: Nothing — this is a review-only bead with no implementation work.
handoff_summary.risks: None identified. The implementation is clean and complete.
handoff_summary.next_action: Bead is ready to complete. No follow-up work required.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: project.pbxproj was regenerated by xcodegen inside this worktree; any downstream bead that adds source files should re-run xcodegen after merging this feature branch to main to avoid "cannot find X in scope" build errors per CLAUDE.md operational rules.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: ArticleFeed query mapping: .source → source_id, .important/.unread → view param. unread_only param is omitted entirely (not sent as false) when unreadOnly is false. articlesSort and articlesUnreadOnly are global shared preferences (ListPreferences) rather than per-feed, consistent with threadsSort. ArticlePagerView is bounded to the loaded articles slice with no on-demand page loading from within the pager (documented known limitation). importancePill copied verbatim from ArticleDetailView including .foregroundStyle(.white) on colored background — pre-existing pattern, spec-mandated.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Three test cases in ArticleFeedTests cover all spec-required scenarios: feed→query-param mapping for all three cases, getArticles URL composition with cursor percent-encoding, and allowsUnreadFilter for all three feed types. No test for unreadOnly=false omitting unread_only param — implementation is correct (verified in APIClient code) and this case is not explicitly required by the spec test description.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: ArticlePagerView is bounded to the already-loaded articles slice; swiping past the last loaded article stops — the user must return to the list and scroll to load more before re-entering the pager. This is documented in the spec as a future enhancement.

conflict_risks: project.pbxproj was regenerated by xcodegen inside this worktree; any downstream bead that adds source files should re-run xcodegen after merging this feature branch to main to avoid "cannot find X in scope" build errors per CLAUDE.md operational rules.

execution_history.summary: All changed files reviewed and approved — implementation correctly satisfies every acceptance criterion in the spec with no unresolved findings.

guardrails.template_text:
# Review Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Inspect code, tests, docs, and acceptance criteria for correctness, completeness, and risk.

Allowed actions:
- Review only the changed files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read unrelated files.
- Validate acceptance criteria against the implementation and handoff state.
- Block with a clear recommendation when the bead actually requires implementation work.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before reviewing any files, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries to inform the review; skip entries that don't apply.

Do **not** write to memory — review agents are read-only.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.
- Focus on correctness, completeness, and risk — not style or formatting.
- Keep the review concise. If there are no findings, say so and approve promptly.

Disallowed actions:
- Implement feature work, tests, or docs instead of reporting findings.
- Rewrite architecture or silently fix issues discovered during review.
- Mark incomplete work as accepted without evidence.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (reviews always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of the review result.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the review signoff decision: `approved` means the bead can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when no unresolved findings remain.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved finding count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when any required fix remains.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Review findings ordered by severity, or an explicit statement that no findings were discovered.
- Clear blocked handoff details when the task belongs to another agent type.
